title: Guess The James Cameron Movie From Just 3 Emoji (This Is Harder Than It Looks)
dek: The king of the world has directed some of the biggest movies ever made — see if you can crack all eight before you scroll to the fun facts.

items.reveal_text: Arnold Schwarzenegger originally read for hero Kyle Reese, but during a lunch meeting he described the Terminator's robotic movement in such detail that Cameron cast him as the killer machine instead — on a scrappy $6.4 million budget.
T2's roughly $100 million budget made it the priciest movie ever made at the time, and its opening three minutes alone cost more than the entire original Terminator.

Cameron famously locked in the pitch by writing 'ALIEN' on a whiteboard and slashing the final letter into a dollar sign for Fox executives, and he leaned hard into a Vietnam War allegory, even basing the dropship design on real Huey and Cobra helicopters.

Cameron shot the underwater scenes inside an abandoned, unfinished nuclear reactor containment vessel in Gaffney, South Carolina, flooding it with over 7 million gallons of water for what became one of the most grueling shoots of his career.

True Lies became the first movie ever to carry a nine-figure production budget, and its climactic Harrier jet stunt used three real AV-8B jump jets rented from the Marine Corps for over $2,400 an hour.
Cameron had a nearly full-scale replica of the ship built at a new studio in Rosarito, Mexico, inside a 17-million-gallon tank, after his budget ballooned to a then-record $200 million.

Cameron wrote his original 80-page Avatar treatment in 1994 but shelved the project for over a decade, waiting until visual effects breakthroughs on films like Gollum and King Kong convinced him the technology could finally match his vision.

The cast trained for months in freediving so they could perform real underwater takes without bubbles ruining the motion-capture, and Kate Winslet stunned the crew by holding her breath for over seven minutes straight.
